title: Reviewer findings (PRD §7, FR-5.2)
description: Structured output of an adversarial review step. The `findings` array is normative per PRD §7. `open_questions` and `summary` are P4 schema additions (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #5): reviewers naturally emit questions that are not claims, and forcing them through the verdict enum is wrong — they get their own slot and the triager handles them first-class. The PRD §7 excerpt itself is unchanged. Strict-mode note (pinned, codex 0.139.0): native structured output requires EVERY property in `required`, so §7-optional fields are spelled required-but-nullable; `suggested_fix: null` means 'none offered'.

id.description: Stable finding ID, e.g. F-001; referenced by triage and confirm verdicts. Engine-synthesized findings (e.g. a reviewer-mutation record) carry suffixed IDs like F-R1-MUTATION.

suggested_fix.description: Optional per §7 — null when the reviewer offers none.

open_questions.description: Questions, not claims (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #5). Triage treats each as data and may convert one into a finding.
